paper.title_en: Development of a quartz-based threshold Cherenkov Veto counter with MPPC Readout for a Hadron spectroscopy experiment at J-PARC
paper.abstract_en: We have developed a synthetic fused-silica based Kaon Veto Cherenkov counter (KVC) for the J-PARC E72 experiment, which aims to explore hyperon resonances near the Λη threshold via K−p reactions. The KVC is required to reject beam-through kaons in the momentum range of 0.65–0.95 GeV/c, corresponding to the velocity region of β≈0.8–0.9, with high efficiency (>99%) under a 1.0 T magnetic field. A major technical challenge in this velocity region is that the Cherenkov angle is approximately 31°–40° for kaons, which falls below the critical angle for total internal reflection (TIR) at the silica–air interface (θcrit≈43.2°), leading to light loss through the radiator side faces. To overcome this limitation, we applied a ground-glass finish to the radiator side faces to induce diffuse scattering and redirect photons toward the sensor planes. In a beam test at the J-PARC K1.8BR beamline, the ground-glass configuration yielded a mean light yield of approximately 86Np.e., providing a factor-of-1.8 improvement over the 48Np.e. obtained with the conventional polished configuration. This enhancement provided a sufficient safety margin for stable operation. The detector was successfully operated throughout the J-PARC E72 physics run, achieving a detection efficiency of >99% and enabling stable data collection.
